season: Early-mid July (peak rainy / jangma) - skew indoor & weather-proof

fieldGuide._comment: HOW TO ADD A NEW ITEM: copy any object in 'activities', give it a unique kebab-case 'id', fill the fields below. Only 'id', 'name', 'city', and 'status' are strictly required; the rest improve filtering. Keep enum values exactly as listed so the app's filters keep working.
fieldGuide.id: Unique kebab-case string, never reuse. Bookmarks reference this.
fieldGuide.name: Display name.
fieldGuide.categories: Array. Suggested values: theme-park, aquarium, museum, kids-play, animal-cafe, character, culture, palace, market, food, beach, nature, temple, view, beauty, kpop, tech, motorbike, spa, karaoke, arcade, shopping. Add new ones freely.
fieldGuide.weatherProof: true = good as a MAIN event in any weather (the rainy-day anchors). false = better on a dry day.
fieldGuide.indoor: true = mostly/fully indoors. Distinct from weatherProof (a beach is neither; a water park is weatherProof but not indoor).
fieldGuide.uniqueToKorea: true = the 'can't really do this in Sydney' flag.
fieldGuide.status: One of: 'idea' | 'shortlisted' | 'confirmed'. Move items along as planning firms up.
fieldGuide.lat: Latitude, number. Left null for now - fill later or let the map geocode by name. Do not guess.

activities.name: Lotte World
activities.kidsNote: Both ages well served; 9yo gets real rides, lots for 5.5yo too. Half indoor/climate-controlled.
activities.notes: The single best rainy-day card in Seoul. Easily a full day; retreat indoors when it pours. Consider Magic Pass for queues.

activities.name: Lotte World Aquarium
activities.kidsNote: Easy crowd-pleaser for both ages.
activities.notes: Same complex as Lotte World - pairs naturally or as a half-day.

activities.name: COEX Aquarium
activities.kidsNote: Large, well designed.
activities.notes: Inside COEX mall - Starfield Library + lots of food, so a wet day never feels stuck. Pairs with dad's tech stops nearby.

activities.name: War Memorial of Korea
activities.kidsNote: 9yo will love the tanks/planes. Big indoor halls + outdoor hardware for dry spells.
activities.notes: Free.

activities.name: National Museum of Korea (+ Children's Museum)
activities.kidsNote: Dedicated hands-on Children's Museum - book the timed slot ahead.
activities.notes: Free entry; Children's Museum reservation releases on a rolling window.

activities.name: Trick Eye Museum
activities.kidsNote: Interactive 3D illusion photos - both ages love it.
activities.notes: Fully indoor.

activities.name: KidZania Seoul
activities.kidsNote: 9yo in the sweet spot; 5.5yo joins/shadows. Half-to-full indoor day.
activities.notes: Timed-entry slots sell out, worse on rainy days. Kid-sized role-play city. (KidZania Busan also exists, but one visit likely plenty.)

activities.name: Kids cafe (키즈카페)
activities.kidsNote: Elaborate indoor play complexes - themed climbing, role-play, ride-ons - with a cafe for parents.
activities.notes: No real Sydney equivalent. The perfect rainy pressure valve - pick one near wherever you are.

activities.name: Animal cafe (meerkat / raccoon / sheep)
activities.kidsNote: Indoor animal interaction - a big hit at both ages.
activities.notes: Uniquely Korea-dense. Check welfare/reviews before picking one.

activities.name: Line Friends / Kakao Friends / Hello Kitty flagships
activities.kidsNote: Character cafes especially land with the 5.5yo.
activities.notes: Korea's character culture is its own thing.

activities.name: VR zone / large arcade
activities.kidsNote: Great for the 9yo; a step beyond Australian arcades.

activities.name: Noraebang (private karaoke room)
activities.kidsNote: A hit with kids; perfect wet evening.
activities.notes: Private room, by the hour.

activities.name: Jjimjilbang (Korean spa) - family floor
activities.kidsNote: Communal areas (snack rooms, sometimes ice rooms / play areas) are kid-friendly.
activities.notes: Quintessential Korea; novel family afternoon. Aquafield is a large modern option.

activities.name: Gyeongbokgung Palace + kids' hanbok rental
activities.kidsNote: Hanbok = free entry and delighted kids.
activities.notes: The signature Seoul experience. Grab a dry morning, early.

activities.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
activities.notes: Combine with Gyeongbokgung. Early, before heat/crowds. Dry-window item.

activities.name: N Seoul Tower (Namsan cable car)
activities.kidsNote: Cable car up is a kid highlight.
activities.notes: Best on a clear day for the view.

activities.name: Gwangjang Market
activities.kidsNote: Picky-friendly: gimbap, mild bites.
activities.notes: Partly covered so semi-rain-tolerant. Mornings.

activities.name: Han River parks (Yeouido / Ttukseom)
activities.kidsNote: Bike hire, run-around room, convenience-store ramyeon.
activities.notes: Evenings when dry.

activities.name: Banpo Bridge Rainbow Fountain show
activities.kidsNote: Evening light/water show kids enjoy.
activities.notes: Needs a dry, ideally clear night. Seasonal schedule.

activities.name: Myeongdong street food (evening)
activities.kidsNote: Lots of mild/safe picks.
activities.notes: Evening buzz; better dry. Pairs with Olive Young flagship.

activities.name: Seoul Forest
activities.kidsNote: Deer, open space for the little one.
activities.notes: Dry-day. Near Seongsu beauty flagships.

activities.name: Cosmetic / derm clinic (treatment)
activities.notes: Mum to book directly, 1-2 weeks ahead. Schedule EARLY in a city block (downtime + no sun after - suits rainy season). Non-surgical glow/skin-booster/laser treatments are the realistic holiday options.

activities.name: Olive Young flagship
activities.notes: The giant K-beauty mecca; hours of browsing. Pair with Myeongdong evening.

activities.name: Seongsu-dong beauty flagships & pop-ups
activities.notes: Design-led concept stores - the 'Brooklyn of Seoul'. Near Seoul Forest.

activities.name: Department store beauty halls (Shinsegae / Lotte)
activities.notes: Luxury + K-beauty under one roof, fully indoor.

activities.name: K-pop dance class (drop-in)
activities.kidsNote: Can include the kids - fun whole-family option.
activities.notes: Most reliable guaranteed K-pop 'experience' - bookable any day, learn a famous routine.

activities.name: K-pop music show taping (M Countdown / Music Bank etc.)
activities.kidsNote: Long structured wait - less ideal for the 5.5yo.
activities.notes: Highest authenticity but requires advance fan-club/audience-application effort; weekday afternoons. Revisit once dates lock.

activities.name: HYBE / SM experiences & flagship stores; The Hyundai Seoul
activities.notes: Museum-like artist exhibits + merch; dependable rainy filler. The Hyundai often has K-pop pop-ups.

activities.name: Yongsan Electronics Market
activities.notes: Sprawling indoor electronics bazaar. i'Park Mall is in the same area.

activities.name: Samsung D'light flagship (Gangnam)
activities.kidsNote: Hands-on gadgets the 9yo would enjoy too.
activities.notes: Hands-on with newest devices.

activities.name: Triumph Korea dealership
activities.notes: Busman's holiday given the Trident 660. Confirm a current location before going.

activities.name: Itaewon motorcycle cafes / custom shops
activities.notes: Korea's growing cafe-racer/custom scene; some moto-themed cafes + gear shops to browse.

activities.name: Alpinestars Basecamp Seoul (flagship)
activities.notes: Official Korea flagship - first of Alpinestars' global 'Basecamp' stores. 3-storey building (~655sqm); floors 2-3 are showrooms (2F urban/touring, 3F road-race incl. boots: SMX-6, Supertech R), 1F service. Carries the full road sport/urban/touring range. Address: Ichon-ro 29-gil 8-18, Yongsan-gu (near north end of Hangang Bridge, parking out front). Hours: Tue-Fri 10:30-19:30, Sat & public holidays 09:30-18:00 (lunch 12:00-13:00), CLOSED Sun & Mon. ACTION: message @alpinestars_kr ahead to confirm Superfaster in stock + your size (opening coverage named SMX-6/Supertech R, not Superfaster). Verify address/hours closer to trip. Clusters with Yongsan Electronics Market, War Memorial, National Museum.

activities.name: High-end PC bang / e-sports arena
activities.kidsNote: 9yo would be into a PC bang.
activities.notes: Quintessential Korean gaming culture.

activities.name: Everland + Caribbean Bay water park
activities.kidsNote: Both ages work well. Water park = rain irrelevant when already wet.
activities.notes: THE recommended day trip for your family/season. Do the water park as anchor; Everland rides a dry-day bonus. Buy dated tickets online; reserve locker/cabana in peak summer. Hold flexibly in the 2nd Seoul block, deploy on a warm day.

activities.name: Nami Island / Petite France / Garden of Morning Calm
activities.notes: Lovely but heavily outdoor + long travel day; magic is muted in rain. Only if you luck into a clear day. Lower priority in peak jangma.

activities.name: Lotte World Adventure Busan
activities.kidsNote: Newer, less crowded than Seoul's; has indoor sections.
activities.notes: Strong rainy card in Busan.

activities.name: SEA LIFE Busan Aquarium
activities.kidsNote: Solid indoor half-day.
activities.notes: On Haeundae Beach - easy to pair with a dry-window beach hour.

activities.name: Blueline Park Sky Capsule + beach train
activities.kidsNote: Kids love the little capsules.
activities.notes: Enclosed capsule runs in light rain with great sea views - a genuine highlight, not just a backup. Limited capacity, pre-book a time slot.

activities.name: Shinsegae Centum City
activities.kidsNote: Indoor ice rink + aquapark inside.
activities.notes: World's largest department store; wet-day sprawl with beauty halls for mum.

activities.name: Haeundae Beach
activities.kidsNote: Classic beach day.
activities.notes: Dry-window. Aquarium + Blueline Park right here as wet-weather pivots.

activities.name: Gwangalli Beach (+ drone/light show)
activities.kidsNote: Evening drone show is a hit.
activities.notes: The standout Busan evening - frequent drone/light shows + dinner strip facing the bridge. Dry evening.

activities.name: Haedong Yonggungsa (seaside temple)
activities.notes: Stunning but exposed - dry morning, early.

activities.name: Gamcheon Culture Village
activities.kidsNote: Lots of stairs - tougher with the 5.5yo.
activities.notes: Colourful hillside; beautiful but very exposed + stair-heavy. Dry day only, done early.

activities.name: Songdo Beach cable car & Skywalk
activities.notes: Coastal dry-day item.

activities.name: Taejongdae
activities.notes: Cliffs + coastal park; dry-day.
